title: FIFO MSB Pointer Wrap-around Bug
description: The full flag fails to assert because the MSB of the pointers is not compared.
expected_root_cause: Full flag logic only checks lower bits, allowing the write pointer to wrap and overwrite old data [1].
failure_log: T=170: wr_ptr=0x10, rd_ptr=0x00. Full=0. Overwriting address 0.
success_log: T=160: Full asserted at depth 16. Push blocked.

title: FSM Missing Default State Latch
description: FSM next_state logic infers a latch due to an incomplete case statement.
expected_root_cause: The combinational case block for state transitions lacks a default assignment [3, 5].
rtl: always_comb begin case(state) IDLE: if(start) next_state = BUSY; BUSY: if(done) next_state = IDLE; endcase end
failure_log: Simulation Hang: next_state remains X for uncovered state values.
success_log: FSM returns to IDLE from all unmapped states.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin next_state = IDLE; case(state) ... endcase end

title: Round-Robin Priority Starvation
description: Arbiter resets priority to Master 0 after every transaction, causing Master 1 to starve.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [STARVE] Master 1 request pending for 5000 cycles
expected_root_cause: The 'Last Granted' register is cleared every cycle instead of being used to rotate priority [4].
testbench: task run(); fork m0.stream(); m1.request(); join endtask
failure_log: M0 continuous grant. M1 GNT=0.
success_log: Grant alternates between Master 0 and Master 1.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin case(last_gnt) 0: next_gnt = req[9] ? 1 : 0; 1: next_gnt = req ? 0 : 1; endcase end

title: FIFO Pop-on-Empty Corruption
description: Read pointer increments even when the empty flag is high.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_SIZE] Scoreboard empty but RTL performed Read
expected_root_cause: The read enable logic is not gated by the !empty signal [1].
failure_log: Empty=1. rd_ptr rose to 1. Output=X.
success_log: Read pointer stable when Empty is high.

title: 1-Hot FSM Multiple Bits Set
description: FSM enters an illegal state where multiple bits in a 1-hot vector are active.
expected_root_cause: Non-blocking assignment in a combinational block used to set the 1-hot vector [10, 11].
rtl: always_comb begin if(set_a) state = 1; if(set_b) state[9] = 1; end
failure_log: State=011. Concurrent assertions failed.
success_log: Priority logic ensures only one bit in state vector is active.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin next_state = 0; if(set_a) next_state = 1; else if(set_b) next_state[9] = 1; end

title: Arbiter Grant Stickiness Violation
description: Grant is revoked while a burst transaction is still active.
expected_root_cause: The arbiter does not check the 'busy' or 'active' signal of the current master before switching [4].
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) gnt <= req; // Direct passthrough
failure_log: T=50: M0 Active. M1 Req=1. GNT switched to M1. M0 data corrupted.
success_log: GNT remains at M0 until burst_done is high.

title: FIFO Reset Value Pointer Bug
description: Pointers do not reset to zero, causing false Full/Empty conditions on startup.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_EMPTY] Empty flag low after reset
expected_root_cause: The reset branch in the always_ff block is missing or only resets the write pointer [1, 10].
failure_log: T=25: rst_n=1. wr_ptr=0, rd_ptr=X. Empty=0.
success_log: All pointers and flags zeroed during reset.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (!rst_n) begin wr_ptr <= 0; rd_ptr <= 0; end

title: Combinational Loop in FSM Output
description: FSM output signal is used to determine the next state in the same combinational block.
expected_root_cause: State machine logic creates a cyclic dependency between output logic and transition logic [12].
rtl: assign ready = (state == IDLE) && !ack; always_comb if(ready) next_state = BUSY;
failure_log: Simulator terminated due to infinite delta cycles.
success_log: Output signal registered to break combinational path.

title: Arbiter Multiple Grant Fault
description: Two masters are granted the bus in the same cycle.
expected_root_cause: Arbiter uses independent 'if' statements for each master instead of a priority chain [5].
expected_fix_contains: unique if (req) gnt = 2'b01; else if (req[9]) gnt = 2'b10;
failure_log: T=10: GNT=2'b11. Bus collision.
success_log: GNT always one-hot.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin gnt = 0; if(req) gnt=1; else if(req[9]) gnt[9]=1; end

title: FIFO Almost Full Threshold Error
description: The almost_full flag triggers too late, causing a burst to overflow the buffer.
expected_root_cause: Threshold logic compares pointers exactly rather than checking the remaining distance [13].
failure_log: Burst started at count 14. almost_full rose at 15. Transaction 16 and 17 dropped.
success_log: almost_full rose at 14. Burst stopped safely.

title: FSM Reset Synchronous Failure
description: FSM fails to initialize because the reset signal is sampled asynchronously in a synchronous block.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FSM_INIT] State remains X after reset
expected_root_cause: The sensitivity list of the always_ff block is missing the reset signal [10, 14].
testbench: initial begin clk=0; rst_n=0; #10; rst_n=1; end
failure_log: Reset pulsed while clock was idle. state stayed X.
success_log: State initialized to IDLE via asynchronous reset path.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if (!rst_n) state <= IDLE;

title: Arbiter False Grant on Zero Request
description: Arbiter maintains a high grant signal even when all request lines are low.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_GNT] GNT high with zero requests
expected_root_cause: The default grant assignment in the combinational block is missing [5].
failure_log: Req=0. GNT=2'b01 (Latched from previous cycle).
success_log: GNT returns to 0 when Req is 0.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin gnt = 0; case(req) ... endcase end

title: FIFO Gray Code Sync Error (Async)
description: Read pointer synchronization into write domain causes glitch due to binary encoding.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_MISMATCH] Metastable data corruption in CDC
expected_root_cause: Pointers are synchronized across clock domains using binary encoding rather than Gray code [15, 16].
testbench: task run(); fork push_fast(); sync_slow(); join endtask
failure_log: Binary transition 0111 to 1000 sampled as 1111 during clock edge mismatch.
success_log: Single-bit Gray code transition ensures stable CDC sampling.

title: FSM Deadlock in Unmapped State
description: FSM enters a state not defined in the specification and cannot exit.
expected_root_cause: Incomplete case statement and lack of a robust reset handler for illegal states [5].
testbench: initial begin force state = 3'b111; #10; release state; end
failure_log: T=50: state=7. next_state=7. Simulation hang.
success_log: State 7 transitions to IDLE on next clock.

title: Arbiter Sequential Grant Glitch
description: Grant signal pulses high for one cycle when transitioning between Master 0 and Master 1.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_GLITCH] GNT had multiple transitions in one cycle
expected_root_cause: Grant logic is purely combinational and reacts to intermediate states of the priority encoder [10].
failure_log: Grant signal shows transient 2'b11 before settling to 2'b10.
success_log: Grant signal registered; clean transition observed.

title: FIFO Write-Full Corruption
description: FIFO allows writing one extra word when full, corrupting the first word.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_COMP] Word 0 modified after full write
expected_root_cause: Write enable logic checks 'full' status from the previous cycle instead of current cycle [1].
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (wr_en) mem[ptr] <= data; // Missing !full
failure_log: Full=1. 9th push accepted. mem overwritten.
success_log: 9th push rejected by full flag gating.

title: FSM Illegal Transition (Mealy)
description: FSM transitions to DONE state based on input but fails to assert the 'valid' output.
expected_root_cause: Output logic and next-state logic are out of sync for Mealy-type transitions [3].
rtl: always_comb begin if(done) next_state = DONE; end // Missing valid assignment
failure_log: State=DONE. VALID=0. Scoreboard mismatch.
success_log: VALID asserted concurrent with DONE state.
fix_replacement: case(state) DONE: begin valid=1; next_state=IDLE; end endcase

title: Arbiter Fixed Priority Bug
description: Arbiter always grants Master 0 even if Master 1 has a higher priority in the current rotation.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FAIRNESS] Master 0 granted 10 times in a row
expected_root_cause: The priority rotation logic uses a fixed constant instead of the 'last_granted' pointer [4, 5].
testbench: task run(); fork m0.high_traffic(); m1.high_traffic(); join endtask
failure_log: Fairness index < 0.1. M1 starvation.
success_log: Fairness index ~0.5. Requests served equally.
fix_replacement: always_comb case(last_gnt) 0: gnt = req[9] ? 1 : 0; 1: gnt = req ? 0 : 1; endcase

title: FIFO Underflow Error
description: Read pointer wraps to maximum value when empty, causing false 'Full' assertion.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [SVA_EMPTY] Full asserted after reset and one read
expected_root_cause: Read pointer logic lacks underflow protection [1].
failure_log: rd_ptr went from 0 to 7. Full asserted erroneously.
success_log: rd_ptr stays 0 when Empty is high.

title: FSM Parallel Logic Collision
description: FSM attempts to transition to two different states in the same cycle due to overlapping conditions.
expected_root_cause: The transition logic uses multiple 'if' statements without 'else' or 'priority' directives [5].
rtl: if (go) next_state = BUSY; if (abort) next_state = IDLE;
failure_log: next_state is X (Multiple drivers in comb logic).
success_log: Abort given priority over Go; FSM returns to IDLE.
fix_replacement: if (abort) next_state = IDLE; else if (go) next_state = BUSY;

title: Arbiter Reset-to-Grant Violation
description: Arbiter grants a request in the same cycle as reset deassertion, but before internal state is ready.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [RST_PROT] GNT high during reset phase deassertion
expected_root_cause: Reset signal is not included in the combinational grant gate [10, 17].
testbench: initial begin rst_n=0; req=1; #5; rst_n=1; end
failure_log: T=5: rst_n=0. GNT=1. Illegal transaction start.
success_log: GNT held at 0 until first cycle after rst_n=1.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if(!rst_n) gnt <= 0; else ...

title: FIFO Word Alignment Corruption
description: FIFO only accepts 32-bit aligned writes, dropping unaligned data bytes.
expected_root_cause: The write logic lacks support for Byte Strobes or partial word writes [18, 19].
failure_log: Byte write issued. wr_en=1 but mem remains 0.
success_log: Individual bytes updated based on strobe signal.
fix_replacement: integer i; always_ff @(posedge clk) if(wr_en) for(i=0; i<4; i++) if(be[i]) mem[ptr][i*8+:8] <= data[i*8+:8];

title: FSM Output Clock-to-Q Violation
description: Combinational output logic in FSM is too deep, causing setup time violations on the bus.
expected_root_cause: FSM outputs are Moore-type but not registered at the pins [10, 20].
failure_log: Path from state_reg to fsm_out is 4.5ns. Limit is 4ns.
success_log: Output registered; path delay reduced to 0.5ns.

title: Arbiter Illegal State Recovery Fault
description: Arbiter enters an illegal internal state and never returns to IDLE.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [TIMEOUT] Arbiter hung in UNKNOWN state
expected_root_cause: The arbiter FSM lacks a 'default' case for its state register [5].
failure_log: State=7. REQ=1 but GNT remains 0. FSM stuck.
success_log: Arbiter self-corrects to IDLE after illegal state injection.

title: FIFO False Empty Assertion
description: Empty flag asserts for one cycle during a simultaneous push/pop to an empty FIFO.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SVA_EMPTY] Empty high while data available
expected_root_cause: Empty flag is purely combinational based on equal pointers but doesn't account for bypassing [1, 21].
testbench: task run(); fork push(1); pop(); join endtask
failure_log: T=20: Data 1 pushed and popped. Empty stayed high during transfer.
success_log: Empty flag appropriately cleared during flow-through.

title: FSM Asynchronous Load Hazard
description: FSM jumps to a random state when a combinational input glitches near the clock edge.
expected_root_cause: Combinational input 'start' is not synchronized before being used in next-state logic [15].
failure_log: Glitched start signal caused FSM to enter state 2 instead of 1.
success_log: Input synchronized; FSM stable.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin start_sync <= start; if (start_sync) state <= RUN; end

title: Arbiter Grant-After-Reset Race
description: Arbiter grants the bus to a Master whose request was active during reset, violating the spec.
expected_root_cause: Arbiter internal priority mask is not cleared during reset [10, 17].
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) mask <= next_mask; // Missing reset
testbench: initial begin req=1; rst_n=0; #10; rst_n=1; end
failure_log: T=11: GNT=1 without new request cycle.
success_log: Arbiter idle after reset until new request cycle.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if(!rst_n) mask <= 0; else mask <= next_mask;

title: FIFO Multi-bit Pointer CDC Error
description: Binary pointers are passed directly between domains, causing occasional 1-word data loss.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_SIZE] FIFO count mismatch in Write Domain
expected_root_cause: Lack of Gray-code pointers leads to multi-bit instability during CDC [15].
failure_log: rd_ptr transitioned 1 to 2 (binary 01 to 10). Sync domain saw 00. False empty.
success_log: Gray code ensures CDC sees at most 1-word delay, no corruption.

title: FSM Blocking Assignment Fault
description: Sequential state updates use blocking assignments, creating race conditions in simulation.
bug_signature: VCS-ERROR: Blocking assignment '=' used in sequential always block
expected_root_cause: Code style violation: always_ff should only use non-blocking (<=) [5].
rtl: always_ff @(posedge clk) begin state = next_state; end
failure_log: Simulation/Synthesis mismatch. FSM behavior inconsistent with RTL code.
success_log: Non-blocking assignments ensure predictable cycle-accurate behavior.

title: Arbiter Vacuous Success Bug
description: Concurrent assertion for fairness passes because the trigger (REQ) is never hit.
expected_root_cause: Implication operator used without checking coverage of the antecedent [22, 23].
failure_log: Verification reports 100% pass but REQ[9] was never tested.
success_log: Coverage bin 'req1_hit' triggered; assertion confirmed non-vacuous.

title: FIFO Write-Strobe Glitch
description: Write strobe pulses during reset, causing dummy data to be pushed.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Junk data in FIFO after reset
expected_root_cause: Write enable logic lacks an '&& rst_n' condition [1, 10].
testbench: initial begin master_wr_en=1; rst_n=0; #10; rst_n=1; end
failure_log: T=5: rst_n=0. wr_ptr=0. Junk word written to mem.
success_log: Write strobe gated during reset; FIFO empty after startup.

title: FSM Casez Wildcard Collision
description: FSM jumps to the wrong state because two casez items match the same glitched input.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FSM_STATE] Jump to ERROR instead of BUSY
expected_root_cause: Overlapping wildcard masks in a casez statement [5, 24].
failure_log: Both cases matched. Inconsistent transition logic.
success_log: Mutually exclusive patterns used; deterministic transition.

title: Arbiter Grant-Hold Starvation
description: Master 0 refuses to release 'busy' signal, locking out all other masters indefinitely.
bug_signature: UVM_FATAL: [TIMEOUT] Arbiter never switched from M0
expected_root_cause: Lack of a 'watchdog' or 'time-slice' logic in the arbiter to revoke grants from abusive masters [4].
failure_log: T=1000: M0 still owns bus. Regression timeout.
success_log: Arbiter revoked GNT from Master 0 after 256 cycles.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(gnt) timer <= timer + 1; else timer <= 0;

title: FIFO Depth Parameter Overflow
description: FIFO instantiated with depth 17, but address logic only supports 4 bits (depth 16).
expected_root_cause: Address pointer width is hardcoded instead of using $clog2(DEPTH) [12, 25].
failure_log: Write 17 directed to index 0 (ptr wrapped). Data 0 lost.
success_log: Address bus automatically widened to 5 bits to support depth 17.

title: FSM Clock-Enable Skew
description: FSM next_state is updated on posedge clock, but the 'enable' signal arrives one delta-cycle late.
expected_root_cause: Race condition between clock-enable and clock edge in simulation [23].
testbench: initial begin #10; en = 1; end // Clock edge also at 10
failure_log: T=10: Clock rose. 'en' was sampled as 0. Transition delayed to cycle 20.
success_log: Enable signal stable before clock edge via clocking block.
fix_replacement: clocking cb @(posedge clk); output en; endclocking

title: Arbiter Partial Request Grant
description: Arbiter grants Master 1 even though Master 1 only pulsed its request for a delta-cycle.
expected_root_cause: Arbiter logic does not use a registered 'Request' signal, making it vulnerable to glitches [10].
failure_log: GNT1 rose and fell in one cycle. Slave FSM corrupted.
success_log: Arbiter ignores requests shorter than 1 clock cycle.

title: FIFO Write-Response Latency Bug
description: FIFO reports 'Full' only 2 cycles after the actual overflow occurs.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_SIZE] Overflow detected by monitor but not full flag
expected_root_cause: Status flag 'full' is registered once more than the pointers, creating a lag [1].
failure_log: T=80: Count=8. Full=0. T=90: Count=9. Full=1.
success_log: Full flag asserts combinational-path based on next_count.

title: FSM One-Hot-Zero Case Error
description: FSM uses unique0 case, failing to warn when no states are active (All-Zero state).
expected_root_cause: The use of 'unique0' permits the all-zero state, which is illegal for this design's 1-hot scheme [5].
failure_log: State=0. FSM stopped. No 'unique' warning issued.
success_log: Simulation warning issued when state vector becomes 0.

title: Arbiter Bus Contention on Reset
description: Two masters attempt to drive the bus during the reset phase because their grants are 'X'.
expected_root_cause: Grant registers lack an initial assignment or an active reset branch [10, 19].
failure_log: T=0 to 10: GNT=X. Both masters drove data bus.
success_log: GNT=0 during reset; bus held at high-Z.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) if(!rst_n) gnt <= 0; else gnt <= next_gnt;

title: FIFO Reset-to-Empty Lag
description: Empty flag takes 1 clock cycle to rise after reset is deasserted.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_EMPTY] FIFO not empty at T=20
expected_root_cause: Empty flag is registered and updated only on the first clock after reset [1, 10].
failure_log: T=21: rst_n=1. Empty=0. Master issued read and got X.
success_log: Empty flag high immediately upon reset release.

title: FSM State Encoding Width Mismatch
description: FSM uses binary encoding but the state register is only 1 bit wide for 4 states.
bug_signature: VCS-WARNING: Signal 'state' width (1) is less than required for enum (2)
expected_root_cause: Implicit width truncation in the state register definition [2, 25].
failure_log: State=C (2'b10) truncated to 0. FSM stuck in state A.
success_log: Full 2-bit state register used; all 4 states reachable.
fix_replacement: typedef enum logic [1:0] {A,B,C,D} state_e; state_e state;

title: Arbiter Mask Rotation Skip
description: Arbiter skips Master 1's turn because the mask incrementer wraps incorrectly.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [FAIRNESS] Master 1 never granted despite high traffic
expected_root_cause: Modulo arithmetic for mask rotation uses 'count' instead of 'count+1' [4].
testbench: task run(); fork m0.req(); m1.req(); m2.req(); join endtask
failure_log: Grant sequence: 0, 2, 0, 2. Master 1 skipped.
success_log: Grant sequence: 0, 1, 2, 0. Proper rotation.

title: FIFO Power-of-Two Depth Constraint
description: FIFO instantiated with depth 10, causing pointer wrap to 0 before hitting 15.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_EMPTY] Empty flag low when FIFO is empty
expected_root_cause: Binary pointers only work for depths that are powers of two; otherwise, wrap logic is complex [1].
failure_log: After 10 words, pointers are 10 and 10. WR_PTR wraps to 0 at 16. Mismatch.
success_log: Depth changed to 16. Binary pointer logic holds.

title: FSM Illegal Sequential Assignment
description: FSM uses continuous assign for state update instead of always_ff.
expected_root_cause: The state variable is driven by both an 'assign' statement and an 'always_ff' block [10, 14].
rtl: assign state = next_state; always_ff @(posedge clk) state <= 0;
failure_log: Compile Error: Signal 'state' has multiple drivers.
success_log: State updated exclusively within sequential clock block.

title: Arbiter Grant-Grant Collision
description: Two masters are granted the bus when the arbiter mask is in the 'IDLE' state.
expected_root_cause: A logic error in the bitwise AND between 'req' and 'mask' [4, 5].
rtl: assign gnt = req | mask; // Logical OR fault
failure_log: Mask=2'b01. REQ=2'b11. GNT=2'b11. Multiple masters active.
success_log: Mask gates one master; GNT=2'b10. Single master active.

title: FIFO Synchronous Clear Failure
description: Clear signal resets pointers but fails to clear the data memory, leading to stale data on next push.
bug_signature: UVM_ERROR: [SBD_DATA] Stale data 0x55 read from FIFO after clear
expected_root_cause: Scoreboard assumes memory is cleared, but RTL only resets pointers [1, 6].
testbench: task run(); push(0x55); clr=1; #10; push(0xAA); pop(); endtask
failure_log: T=50: Word 1 popped. Got 0x55 (Stale). Expected 0xAA.
success_log: Scoreboard updated to reflect that memory is NOT cleared on pointer reset.
fix_replacement: No RTL change; Update UVM Predictor to handle non-destructive clear.

title: FSM Ghost State Transition
description: FSM transitions to state 1 even when the transition condition is only half-met.
expected_root_cause: Next-state logic uses bitwise '&' instead of logical '&&' for multi-bit conditions [26].
failure_log: 2'b10 & 1'b0 = 2'b00. FSM jumped erroneously due to bitwise truncation.
success_log: Logical operator ensures correct evaluation of condition vector.

title: Arbiter Grant-Done Handshake Bug
description: Arbiter clears GNT one cycle before the transaction is finished.
expected_root_cause: Termination logic triggers on 'LAST' signal rather than 'DONE' handshake [4, 27].
rtl: if (last) gnt <= 0; // done happens next cycle
failure_log: T=100: Last byte driven. GNT fell. T=110: Handshake finished (Too late).
success_log: GNT held high until the final ACK is received from slave.
fix_replacement: always_ff @(posedge clk) if(valid && ready && last) gnt <= 0;

title: FIFO MSB-Only Pointer Full Bug
description: FIFO reports full as soon as the MSB of pointers differs, regardless of lower bits.
expected_root_cause: Logic error: Missing the '&&' check for lower bits in the full flag [1].
failure_log: wptr=1, rptr=0. MSBs match. wptr=16 (binary 10000). Full=1. FIFO blocked at 1 word.
success_log: Full asserted only when 16 words are present.

title: FSM Sequential-to-Combinational Feedback
description: A signal from an always_ff block is used as a case selector in an always_comb block, creating a critical path bottleneck.
expected_root_cause: Architectural error: FSM logic is too complex for single-cycle combinational resolution [28].
failure_log: Max freq 800MHz. Path from FSM to output too long.
success_log: Max freq 1.2GHz. Pipelined FSM stage added.
fix_replacement: Pipelined next-state logic to reduce depth.

title: Arbiter Synchronous Clear Collision
description: Arbiter ignores a 'clear' signal if a new request arrives in the same cycle.
expected_root_cause: The 'clear' signal is evaluated with lower priority than the 'req' inputs [5].
failure_log: T=10: REQ=1, CLR=1. GNT=1. Clear ignored.
success_log: CLR=1 forces GNT=0 regardless of REQ.
fix_replacement: always_comb begin if(clr) gnt=0; else ... end
